 {"id":"bd-xy3rm","title":"Reject trailing btrfs inode item payload bytes","description":"parse_inode_item accepts payload bytes beyond the fixed 160-byte btrfs_inode_item layout. INODE_ITEM is fixed-size, so trailing data can hide malformed leaf payloads. Reject non-exact inode item sizes, add unit/golden coverage, and refresh parity coverage docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T17:16:10.159126812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T17:22:02.605341664Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T17:22:02.603301682Z","close_reason":"Rejected trailing bytes after fixed-size btrfs INODE_ITEM payloads and added unit/golden coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance"]}
 {"id":"bd-xzn0","title":"Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)","description":"# Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)

## GOAL
Replace traditional free-space bitmaps with succinct data structures (rank/select bitmaps) that support O(1) rank and select queries while using near-optimal space.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 7.1: Succinct bitmaps (Jacobson 1989, Clark 1996) store bitmaps in n + o(n) bits while supporting:
- rank(i): count set bits in positions [0, i) — O(1)
- select(k): find position of k-th set bit — O(1)

For free-space bitmaps, this enables:
- O(1) "how many free blocks in range?" (rank)
- O(1) "find k-th free block" (select) — replaces linear scan for allocation

## DESIGN
- Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) + blocks (every 2^9 bits)
- Select via rank binary search or Vigna's select structure
- Space overhead: ~6.25% over raw bitmap (vs 0% for plain bitmap, but queries are O(n))
- Integration: replace ffs-ext4 block bitmap and inode bitmap

## APPLICABILITY
- Optimization for large filesystems where bitmap scans are expensive
- Most impactful when block groups are large or filesystem is >90% full
- Could also accelerate mballoc buddy allocator queries

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select
- [ ] Space overhead < 10% over raw bitmap
- [ ] Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for block/inode bitmaps","acceptance_criteria":"Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select operations. Space overhead below 10% over raw bitmap. Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) plus blocks (every 2^9 bits). Drop-in replacement for block and inode bitmaps via BlockBitmap trait. Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap at 50%, 80%, 95% fullness. Benchmark: rank/select latency vs linear scan. Unit tests: rank correctness for all-zeros, all-ones, random patterns. Unit tests: select correctness for sparse and dense bitmaps. Unit tests: round-trip serialization/deserialization. Property test: rank(select(k)) == k for all valid k.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:45.958783809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:30:55.736127822Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:30:55.736029688Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datastructure","freespace","succinct"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xzn0","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.714682331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-xzn0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-xzo05","title":"Reject zero-length btrfs ROOT_REF names","description":"parse_root_ref accepts name_len=0 and returns an empty subvolume/snapshot name, while ROOT_REF payloads encode the directory entry name linking a parent subvolume to a child. Reject zero root_ref.name_len, add focused adversarial and golden coverage, and update parity docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:30:48.205743576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:35:11.465631519Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:35:11.463736469Z","close_reason":"Rejected zero-length btrfs ROOT_REF names and added focused parser/golden coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance"]}
 {"id":"bd-y06g","title":"Fix FUSE FIEMAP ioctl header layout and add coverage","description":"Current working-tree FIEMAP support in crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs parses and writes struct fiemap header fields at the wrong offsets. Fix Linux header layout handling and add unit coverage for request parsing/response encoding.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BoldSwan","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:22:20.173307688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:31:17.172825808Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:31:17.172606734Z","close_reason":"Fixed FUSE FIEMAP header offsets, added unit coverage, and repaired associated ffs-core compile typo","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fiemap","fuse","ioctl"]}
 {"id":"bd-y1dj","title":"Add btrfs COW delete allocator-failure mutation coverage","description":"Apply testing-fuzzing fallback to the remaining in-memory btrfs COW tree delete mutation paths: add adversarial allocator-failure coverage for delete rebalancing/root-normalization paths, prove failed deletes do not publish deferred frees for still-reachable nodes or change visible tree contents, and update conformance/COVERAGE.md with the new mutation coverage evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T11:44:36.934280526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T11:53:15.343218917Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T11:53:15.340954290Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs COW delete allocator-failure mutation coverage; focused tests, workspace check, workspace clippy, fmt, diff check, and UBS critical scan passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mutation","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-y2oby","title":"add mounted-path read-only flush success coverage for ext4 and btrfs","description":"Survey finding: writable ext4 and btrfs flush paths are covered in fuse_e2e, and ffs-core explicitly allows flush on read-only mounts, but the mounted-path read-only flush contract is not frozen. Scope: add ext4 and btrfs read-only probes that open existing files, call flush via std::io::Write::flush and close, and assert success with unchanged file contents and no implied durability claims. Acceptance: FUSE E2E proves read-only flush succeeds as a non-durable lifecycle hook on both filesystems.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:33:59.236121929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:23:15.409693132Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:23:15.407835938Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@
 | btrfs internal node parsing | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `parse_internal_items` in `ffs-ondisk` |
 | btrfs sys_chunk mapping | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `map_logical_to_physical` in `ffs-ondisk`, with proptest + metamorphic coverage in `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` for translation covariance, chunk-order permutation invariance, and Single-vs-DUP primary mapping equivalence. |
 | btrfs read-only tree walk | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `walk_tree` in `ffs-btrfs` |
-| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_refs`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_xattr_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, rejects impossible tree levels, and rejects partial extension-field fragments; `parse_root_ref()` rejects ROOT_REF payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_inode_refs()`, `parse_dir_items()`, and `parse_xattr_items()` reject zero-length names; `parse_dir_items()` rejects nonzero `data_len` payloads that belong to xattr-shaped items instead of directory entries; `parse_inode_item()` rejects payloads that are not the fixed 160-byte `btrfs_inode_item` size and rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values, nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields, and trailing bytes after fixed regular/prealloc payloads instead of treating encoded or overlong payloads as raw readable data. |
+| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_refs`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_xattr_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, rejects impossible tree levels, and rejects partial extension-field fragments; `parse_root_ref()` rejects zero-length names and trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_inode_refs()`, `parse_dir_items()`, and `parse_xattr_items()` reject zero-length names; `parse_dir_items()` rejects nonzero `data_len` payloads that belong to xattr-shaped items instead of directory entries; `parse_inode_item()` rejects payloads that are not the fixed 160-byte `btrfs_inode_item` size and rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values, nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields, and trailing bytes after fixed regular/prealloc payloads instead of treating encoded or overlong payloads as raw readable data. |
 | btrfs open/validate pipeline | `fs/btrfs/disk-io.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext` in `ffs-core` |
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |

@@ -70,7 +70,7 @@ None. All identified on-disk structures have conformance fixtures.
 | Btrfs chunk-tree items | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover valid multi-stripe chunk-tree item parsing, fixed header truncation, declared stripe payload truncation, trailing payload rejection after declared stripes, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, zero io_align/io_width/sector_size geometry fields, zero stripes, zero stripe device IDs, and multiple RAID profile bit rejection |
 | Btrfs dev items | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover full field-layout parsing, max numeric/classification values, trailing bytes after the fixed 98-byte item, zero device ID rejection, zero-capacity device rejection, impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting, and truncated payload rejection |
 | Btrfs superblocks | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover valid superblock-region parsing, image-offset parsing, bad magic, zero and non-power-of-two sizing fields, oversized sector/node/stripe sizing fields, zero-capacity, zero-device, and `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting rejection, unsupported checksum types, oversized sys_chunk_array declarations, invalid root/chunk/log tree levels, and short region/image rejection |
-| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, zero-length name rejection for INODE_REF/DIR_ITEM/XATTR_ITEM, ROOT_ITEM partial extension-field rejection, nonzero DIR_ITEM data_len rejection, fixed-size INODE_ITEM trailing-byte rejection, fixed-size regular EXTENT_DATA trailing-byte rejection, unsupported extent types, compression values, encoding fields, invalid inode timestamp nanoseconds, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
+| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, zero-length name rejection for ROOT_REF/INODE_REF/DIR_ITEM/XATTR_ITEM, ROOT_ITEM partial extension-field rejection, nonzero DIR_ITEM data_len rejection, fixed-size INODE_ITEM trailing-byte rejection, fixed-size regular EXTENT_DATA trailing-byte rejection, unsupported extent types, compression values, encoding fields, invalid inode timestamp nanoseconds, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
 | Btrfs send streams | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover command CRC32C validation, missing-END rejection, unknown command fallback, zero-length and unknown attributes, multi-attribute commands, trailing partial command headers, and END payload TLV validation |
 | Btrfs delayed refs | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover zero-limit flush preservation, bounded partial flush accounting, delete-underflow queue preservation, and sequence-ordered drain across extent key order |
 | Btrfs transactions | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial mutation tests cover same-tree root replacement, delayed-ref commit failure nonvisibility, and tree-root address overflow nonvisibility |
